commands.review_summary:
The PR materializes non-workspace skills for `rw` sandboxes into a sandbox-owned read-only skills workspace and threads that path through sandbox backends, read guards, prompt rebuilding, and tests.

PR surface: Source +483, Tests +574. Total +1057 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main skips skill sync for `rw` sandbox layout and preserves host-path snapshots for `rw` runs. I did not run a live Docker or SSH reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human maintainer/security review and live behavior proof; I did not find a narrow code defect for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(agents): materialize sandbox skills for rw sandboxes
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes `workspaceAccess: "rw"` sandboxes so non-workspace skills are materialized into a sandbox-readable, read-only skills workspace instead of advertising host paths the agent cannot read.
- Threads the materialized skills workspace through Docker, browser, SSH, local/remote fs bridges, read-tool workspace guards, embedded run prompts, and compaction prompt rebuilds.
- Keeps generated skills out of user-owned project workspace paths, including shared `rw` sandboxes, while preserving workspace-owned `.openclaw/sandbox-skills` content.
- Intentionally out of scope: changing sandbox policy semantics, broad skill loader refactors, or weakening workspace-only write/edit/apply_patch boundaries.
- Review focus: sandbox boundary/read-only behavior, shared `rw` path stability, prompt path rewriting, and remote eligibility filtering.

## Linked context

Closes #90410

Related: closed/unmerged PR #48034 attempted this area but did not make the sync reachable.

This was picked up from the specific-issue queue after ClawSweeper noted: no current fix is present, the related closed PR was unmerged, and the best path is a scoped maintainer-reviewed sandbox/skills fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: In `workspaceAccess: "rw"`, sandboxed agents could see non-workspace skill host paths in the prompt but could not read them because workspace-only/sandbox path guards rejected those paths.
- Real environment tested: Local Windows worktree, Node/pnpm test lanes; mocked sandbox/Docker/SSH/fs bridge tests exercise the affected runtime boundaries.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Tests and validation below.
- Evidence after fix: Focused regression tests now pass for materializing skills outside the user workspace, mounting them read-only into `/workspace/.openclaw/sandbox-skills/skills`, rewriting prompt-facing paths to container-readable locations, preserving remote eligibility, and refreshing SSH materialized skills on fresh and reused runtimes.
- Observed result after fix: Sandboxed `rw` runs rebuild skills from the materialized sandbox skills workspace, advertise container-visible skill paths, and allow read-only reads without allowing writes/edits or deleting user-owned workspace shadows.
- What was not tested: A live Docker/SSH/Crabbox end-to-end run with real external managed/bundled skills.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Mantis was unavailable in this session. Crabbox/Testbox proof was attempted but unavailable: Testbox path failed due missing `blacksmith`; AWS Crabbox lease failed with HTTP 502 (`run_6664098c583b`, slug `brisk-shrimp`). Direct `codex review --base origin/main` repeatedly timed out, so I used the repo-local structured autoreview helper with Codex engine.
- Before evidence: Issue #90410 includes the source-level failing behavior; the added tests encode the missing `rw` sandbox skill visibility path and workspace shadow/data-loss cases.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/sandbox-skills.test.ts src/skills/loading/workspace-load.test.ts src/skills/runtime/embedded-run-entries.test.ts src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/ssh-backend.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-skills-bridge-readonly.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts src/skills/loading/workspace-sync.test.ts src/skills/loading/workspace-bundled-allowlist.test.ts src/skills/loading/workspace-precedence.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:core
corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm oxlint <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/check-changed.mjs --dry-run --base origin/main
PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added/updated:

- `rw` sandbox skill sync target uses sandbox-owned materialized workspace, not user workspace.
- Shared `rw` sandbox materialization uses a stable sandbox state path and preserves user-owned `.openclaw/sandbox-skills` content.
- Docker/browser/read-only mount state includes materialized skills.
- Local and remote fs bridges protect materialized skill roots as read-only.
- SSH backend uploads/refetches materialized skills for fresh and reused runtimes.
- Sandbox prompt rebuild drops host snapshots, loads workspace-only materialized skills, rewrites prompt paths to `/workspace/.openclaw/sandbox-skills/...`, and preserves remote eligibility filtering.
- Read-tool guard normalizes container paths before choosing read-only overlay mounts.

What failed before this fix: `rw` sandboxes had no readable copy/mount for non-workspace skills, so prompt-advertised skill host paths escaped sandbox/workspace roots.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, sandbox file visibility changes for skill files.

Highest-risk area: sandbox filesystem boundaries for read-only skill materialization.

Risk mitigation: generated skills are mounted/read through explicit read-only/protected skill roots; write/edit/apply_patch boundaries remain workspace-only; shared `rw` materialization lives in sandbox state rather than user project paths; Codex autoreview clean after addressing accepted findings.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review, security-sensitive sandbox review, and CI.

Still waiting on: GitHub CI/ClawSweeper after PR creation; optional live Crabbox proof if maintainers want remote environment evidence once Crabbox/Testbox is healthy.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: Codex autoreview findings addressed during local review:

- shared `rw` data-loss risk from syncing into user-owned `.openclaw/sandbox-skills`
- shared container stale bind risk from agent-specific materialized paths
- read guard false denial from matching overlays before normalizing container paths

Final Codex autoreview result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported; overall patch is correct (0.83).
